methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Base Power, a Series C climate and energy company founded by Zach Dell and Justin Lopas, is evaluated against a peer set of 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private—including KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy at the same stage.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $1.0B total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.7x multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $1.7B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables (KoBold Metals at $2.0B, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B, Amogy at $700M) produces a $1.0B median, with a range of $700M to $2.0B. The stage-progression method assumes capital raised correlates with value creation; peer-median reflects realized market consensus but relies on a limited three-company sample.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $850M to $2.0B, with a median of $1.4B. This midpoint reflects reasonable tension between the stage-progression step-up and peer-market positioning. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three peer comparables in this early-stage, capital-intensive sector and whether total raised adequately proxies pre-Series C value. The $1.15B spread between low and high estimates reflects material sensitivity to stage-advancement assumptions and peer selection.
